name: Tibetan sand mandala: a intricate geometric painting made of colored sand, ritually destroyed upon completion. FSPLIT: the monks construct the mandala by placing individual grains, splitting the unified void into the elaborate palace of the deity with its four gates, concentric rings, and central lotus. FFUSE: the destruction ceremony sweeps the mandala into a single pile of mixed sand — reconstituting the multiplicity back into undifferentiated matter, which is then poured into a river. VINIT: the empty platform. TANCH: the outer ring of the mandala — the boundary of the sacred cosmos. AFWD: adding sand (creation). AREV: sweeping sand (dissolution). ENGAGR: the mandala is simultaneously a permanent spiritual reality and a transient material object.

phase_0.boundary_condition: the outer ring of the mandala

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any sand is placed

TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that defines the sacred cosmos boundary

AFWD.justification: forward morphism from void to intricate palace

AREV.justification: reverse/descent from intricate palace to undifferentiated matter

CLINK.justification: composition of individual grain placements into the full mandala

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the mandala as a spiritual reality
IMSCRIB.justification: self-recognition of the mandala's identity as a sacred symbol

FSPLIT.justification: splits the unified void into the elaborate palace with four gates, concentric rings, and central lotus

FFUSE.justification: sweeps the mandala into a single pile of mixed sand, reconstituting multiplicity back into undifferentiated matter

EVALF.justification: negative state where the mandala is not ritually correct

ENGAGR.chosen_element: mandala as permanent spiritual reality and transient material object

IFIX.chosen_element: the memory of the mandala in the monks' minds
IFIX.justification: permanent irreversible record of the mandala's form and ritual

phase_2.fuse_result: single pile of mixed sand (undifferentiated matter)

phase_3.void_description: empty platform before any sand is placed
phase_3.both_description: mandala as both permanent spiritual reality and transient material object

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: monks recognize the empty platform as a sacred space
Step 2 AREV: monks chant and meditate, descending into the void
Step 3 FSPLIT: monks begin placing sand grains, branching the void into the mandala's structure
Step 4 AFWD: monks add more sand, ascending to the full intricate palace
Step 5 FFUSE: destruction ceremony sweeps the mandala into a single pile
Step 6 CLINK: the pile is poured into a river, linking the mandala to the flow of nature
Step 7 IFIX: the memory of the mandala is recorded in the monks' minds
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the monks recognize the cycle as complete, returning to identity

phase_5.memory_mechanism: the sand grains and the monks' mental recollection
phase_5.alfs_store: the first mandala ever created in the tradition

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the sand returns to nature, no net change in matter
